Description: Base substrate for CPUs/GPUs/ASICs used in servers and AI accelerators.
Ultra-pure silicon used to manufacture wafers.
Dopants for forming transistor regions in data-center chips.
Light-sensitive chemicals for lithography of server/accelerator chips.
Gases (N₂, Ar, etc.) used in semiconductor fabrication.
Chemicals for wafer planarization and etching.
High-performance compute dies for AI training/inference.
High-bandwidth stacked DRAM tightly coupled to accelerators.
Organic or silicon interposers connecting dies and HBM.
Tin-silver-copper solder bumps connecting packages to boards.
Epoxy materials stabilizing chip packages under thermal stress.
Printed circuit boards for servers and switches.
Metal enclosures for rack servers.
Rack frames supporting dense AI clusters.
Vertical copper/aluminum busbars delivering DC power in racks.
Mechanical hardware used to mount/slide servers in racks.
DC-DC converters feeding CPUs/GPUs on boards.
Hot-swap AC-DC front-end power supplies feeding rack busbars.
Non-volatile memory packages used in SSDs.
Main memory modules in servers and accelerators.
Spinning disk drives for bulk storage and backups.
Single-mode/multimode fiber for cluster and DC interconnects.
Twisted-pair copper cabling for networking.
Termination hardware for structured cabling.
Pluggable optical modules (100–800G) for high-speed links.
High-radix packet-switching chips in DC fabric switches.
Mechanical and electrical housings for high-port switches.
Step-down transformers at data-center substations.
Grid connection and protection equipment.
Low-voltage distribution within the building.
Overhead busway delivering high currents to racks.
Per-rack power distribution units with monitoring.
Copper grounding for safety and EMI control.
UPS energy storage racks for ride-through and backup.
Rectifiers/inverters and DC-link components in UPS systems.
Backup generation for long-duration outages.
On-site fuel storage for generators.
Room-level air-conditioning/air-handling units.
Sheet-metal air distribution and control devices.
Filtration media for CRAC/CRAH and AHU units.
Large refrigeration units feeding chilled-water loops.
Heat-rejection units for water-cooled plants.
Direct-to-chip cold plates for high-power GPUs/CPUs.
Distribution components for liquid-cooling loops.
Enclosures for dielectric immersion-cooled servers.
Engineered non-conductive fluids for immersion cooling.
High-CFM fans with rare-earth magnets.
Beams and columns for DC building structure.
Foundations and floor slabs supporting heavy equipment.
Reinforcing steel in concrete structures.
Building envelope with insulation for climate control.
Weatherproofing membranes on data-center roofs.
Perforated/solid tiles for airflow and cable routing.
Panels and doors used in hot-/cold-aisle containment.
Chilled-water and condenser-water piping.
Drainage and some cooling-water lines.
Control and isolation valves in cooling and water systems.
Gas or water cylinders for fire protection.
Delivery hardware for water-based fire suppression.
Video monitoring systems for DC security.
Card readers, locks, turnstiles.
High-efficiency luminaires for white space and MEP areas.
Control and monitoring wiring for BMS/SCADA.
Hardware running building management and telemetry.
High-performance magnets in fans, drives, and pumps.
III-V semiconductor materials for optics and power devices.
High-density capacitors on server and switch boards.
High-purity glass for lenses and optical interfaces.
